aws-architect.title: Cloud and AWS Fundamentals
aws-architect.description: Learn the core concepts of cloud computing and the AWS global infrastructure. Understand regions, availability zones, and the shared responsibility model.
topics: Cloud computing models IaaS PaaS SaaS

resources.label: AWS Certified Cloud Practitioner Full Course

aws-architect.challenge: Create an EC2 instance, attach an IAM role with S3 read access, and upload a file to S3 from the instance using the CLI.

aws-architect.title: Networking in AWS
aws-architect.description: Design and build virtual private clouds with public and private subnets. Configure routing, security groups, and network ACLs to control traffic flow.
aws-architect.challenge: Build a 3-tier VPC with public subnets for load balancers, private subnets for app servers, and isolated subnets for databases with correct routing.

aws-architect.title: Compute and Auto Scaling
aws-architect.description: Architect scalable and highly available compute layers using EC2 auto scaling groups and elastic load balancers. Understand horizontal scaling patterns and health checks.
topics: Application Load Balancer vs Network Load Balancer
aws-architect.challenge: Deploy a web application across two availability zones using an ALB, auto scaling group, and a custom scaling policy that triggers at 60 percent CPU.

aws-architect.title: Storage and Database Services
aws-architect.description: Select appropriate storage and database solutions for different workload requirements. Understand consistency models, replication, and backup strategies.
aws-architect.challenge: Design a data tier with RDS Aurora in multi-AZ mode plus a DynamoDB table for session storage, with automated backups and a read replica for reporting.

aws-architect.title: Security and Identity
aws-architect.description: Implement defense-in-depth security using AWS native security services. Apply the principle of least privilege and encrypt data at rest and in transit.
aws-architect.challenge: Set up a security baseline with CloudTrail enabled in all regions, a Config rule that alerts on public S3 buckets, and GuardDuty findings forwarded to a security SNS topic.

aws-architect.title: Infrastructure as Code with CloudFormation
aws-architect.description: Define and manage AWS infrastructure declaratively using CloudFormation templates and the AWS CDK. Implement change sets, nested stacks, and multi-account deployments.
aws-architect.challenge: Create a CloudFormation stack set that deploys a VPC, bastion host, and S3 logging bucket across three AWS accounts with a single command.

aws-architect.title: Serverless and Managed Services
aws-architect.description: Integrate serverless compute with managed messaging and API services to build event-driven architectures. Understand throttling, dead-letter queues, and fan-out patterns.
topics: API Gateway REST and HTTP APIs
aws-architect.challenge: Build an order processing pipeline using API Gateway, Lambda, SQS, and a Step Functions workflow that handles failures and sends status notifications via SNS.

aws-architect.title: High Availability and Disaster Recovery
aws-architect.description: Design systems that meet RTO and RPO targets using AWS global services and cross-region replication strategies. Implement failover routing and content distribution.
topics: Route 53 routing policies and health checks
aws-architect.challenge: Implement a multi-region active-passive setup with Route 53 health-check-based failover, cross-region RDS read replica promotion, and CloudFront for static assets.

aws-architect.title: Cost Optimization and Performance
aws-architect.description: Apply the AWS Well-Architected cost optimization pillar to identify waste and right-size resources. Use savings plans, reserved instances, and spot strategically.
topics: Spot instance strategies and interruption handling
aws-architect.challenge: Audit a simulated AWS account, identify the top three cost drivers, implement savings plan purchases, set up cost allocation tags, and create budget alerts for each team.

aws-architect.title: Advanced Architecture and AWS Professional
aws-architect.description: Design enterprise-grade multi-account AWS landing zones with governance, compliance, and self-service capabilities. Apply all five Well-Architected pillars at scale.
topics: AWS Landing Zone and Control Tower
AWS Service Catalog products and portfolios

resources.label: AWS Solutions Architect Professional Exam Guide
AWS Certified Solutions Architect Study Guide

aws-architect.challenge: Design a full enterprise landing zone with Control Tower, three organizational units, a shared services VPC connected via Transit Gateway, and a Service Catalog portfolio for developer self-service.

azure-architect.title: Azure Fundamentals
azure-architect.description: Understand Azure global infrastructure, core services, and the Azure Resource Manager model. Navigate the portal, CLI, and PowerShell to provision and manage resources.
topics: Azure Free Tier and pricing calculator
azure-architect.challenge: Create a resource group, deploy a Linux VM, attach a managed disk, and configure a network security group to allow SSH only from your IP.

azure-architect.title: Azure Networking
azure-architect.description: Design Azure virtual networks with proper segmentation, routing, and connectivity. Understand hybrid connectivity options including VPN Gateway and ExpressRoute.
topics: Network security groups and application security groups
Azure Load Balancer and Application Gateway
Azure DNS public and private zones
azure-architect.challenge: Build a hub-and-spoke network topology with a shared services hub VNet, two spoke VNets connected via peering, and an Application Gateway in the hub for inbound traffic.

azure-architect.title: Identity and Access with Entra ID
azure-architect.description: Implement enterprise identity management using Microsoft Entra ID including conditional access policies and privileged identity management. Federate external identity providers and manage app registrations.
topics: Entra ID tenants users and groups
Role-based access control and custom roles
azure-architect.challenge: Configure a conditional access policy requiring MFA for admins, enable PIM for the Contributor role in a subscription, and set up a managed identity for a VM to access Key Vault.

azure-architect.title: Compute and Containers
azure-architect.description: Deploy and scale workloads across Azure virtual machine scale sets, App Service plans, and Azure Kubernetes Service. Choose the right compute abstraction for each workload pattern.
topics: Azure App Service tiers and deployment slots
AKS cluster creation and node pools
Azure Functions consumption vs premium plans
azure-architect.challenge: Deploy a containerized API to AKS with horizontal pod autoscaling, a private container registry with managed identity pull access, and an ingress controller with TLS.

azure-architect.title: Storage and Databases
azure-architect.description: Select and configure Azure storage and database services for performance, consistency, and cost. Design geo-redundant and zone-redundant data tiers.
topics: Azure Blob, File, Queue, and Table storage
Cosmos DB APIs and consistency levels
Azure Database for PostgreSQL and MySQL
azure-architect.challenge: Design a data layer with Azure SQL in zone-redundant configuration, Cosmos DB for multi-region writes, and Azure Cache for Redis as a session store with failover configured.

azure-architect.title: Infrastructure as Code with Bicep
azure-architect.description: Author and deploy Azure infrastructure using Bicep templates with modules and parameter files. Integrate IaC into CI/CD pipelines for automated deployments.
topics: Bicep syntax resources modules and outputs
GitHub Actions OIDC authentication to Azure

resources.label: Infrastructure as Code with Azure Bicep

azure-architect.challenge: Write a Bicep module library for a standard Azure landing zone, deploy it across dev and prod subscriptions via GitHub Actions using OIDC federated credentials.

azure-architect.title: Monitoring and Security
azure-architect.description: Build a comprehensive observability and security posture using Azure Monitor, Defender for Cloud, and Microsoft Sentinel. Configure alerting, dashboards, and SIEM rules.
azure-architect.challenge: Deploy a Log Analytics workspace, connect all resources to it, create a KQL alert for failed login attempts, and enable Defender for Cloud with a custom policy initiative.

azure-architect.title: High Availability and Business Continuity
azure-architect.description: Design Azure solutions that meet strict availability SLAs using availability zones, Traffic Manager routing, and Azure Site Recovery for disaster recovery.
topics: Azure Front Door for global HTTP traffic
Azure Site Recovery replication and failover
RTO RPO design for Azure workloads
azure-architect.challenge: Set up Azure Site Recovery for a production VM to a paired region, configure Traffic Manager with performance routing and health probes, and test a full failover drill.

azure-architect.title: Azure Kubernetes Service Advanced
azure-architect.description: Operate production-grade AKS clusters with advanced networking, autoscaling, and security configurations. Integrate Azure services using workload identity and KEDA event-driven scaling.
topics: AKS Azure CNI and Cilium networking
AKS node pool upgrades and maintenance windows
azure-architect.challenge: Configure an AKS cluster with Azure CNI, enable workload identity for a pod that reads from Key Vault, install KEDA with an Azure Service Bus scaler, and enforce admission policies via Azure Policy.

azure-architect.title: Enterprise Architecture and Azure Landing Zones
azure-architect.description: Design and implement enterprise-scale Azure architectures using the Cloud Adoption Framework landing zone accelerators. Govern multi-subscription environments with policy-driven guardrails.
topics: Cost management and chargeback at scale
azure-architect.challenge: Deploy an enterprise-scale landing zone using the ALZ Bicep accelerator with three platform subscriptions, configure Azure Policy initiatives for each, and onboard a workload team via Lighthouse.

gcp-architect.title: GCP Fundamentals
gcp-architect.description: Learn Google Cloud Platform core services, resource hierarchy, and identity management. Use the gcloud CLI and Cloud Console to provision and manage GCP resources.
topics: GCP resource hierarchy projects folders org
Compute Engine VM instances and machine types
Cloud Storage buckets and object lifecycle

resources.label: Google Cloud Skills Boost - Cloud Digital Leader

gcp-architect.challenge: Create a GCP project, enable billing, deploy a Compute Engine VM in a custom VPC, and grant a service account Storage Object Viewer access to a specific bucket.

gcp-architect.title: GCP Networking
gcp-architect.description: Design GCP Virtual Private Cloud networks with shared VPC, VPC peering, and hybrid connectivity. Configure Cloud Load Balancing for global and regional traffic distribution.
topics: GCP VPC and subnets auto vs custom mode
Shared VPC and VPC Network Peering
Cloud Load Balancing types and use cases
Cloud Armor WAF and DDoS protection
gcp-architect.challenge: Set up a shared VPC with a host project and two service projects, deploy an HTTP load balancer with backend instance groups in two regions, and enable Cloud Armor for rate limiting.

gcp-architect.title: Google Kubernetes Engine
gcp-architect.description: Deploy and manage Kubernetes workloads on GKE Standard and Autopilot clusters. Use Workload Identity to securely access GCP services from pods without static credentials.
topics: GKE cluster modes Standard vs Autopilot
GKE Config Connector for GCP resources
gcp-architect.challenge: Deploy a microservices application on GKE Autopilot, configure Workload Identity so each service can only access its own Cloud Storage bucket, and enable Binary Authorization with a manual approval policy.

gcp-architect.title: Data and Analytics with BigQuery
gcp-architect.description: Design data pipelines and analytical workloads using GCP managed data services. Understand BigQuery cost optimization, partitioning, and streaming inserts.
gcp-architect.challenge: Build a streaming analytics pipeline with Pub/Sub as input, Dataflow to transform and enrich events, write to BigQuery with partitioning, and visualize results in Looker Studio.

gcp-architect.title: Security and Compliance
gcp-architect.description: Implement GCP security controls including data encryption, secret management, and perimeter security using VPC Service Controls. Monitor threats with Security Command Center.
topics: Cloud KMS key rings and keys

resources.label: GCP Professional Cloud Security Engineer Guide

gcp-architect.challenge: Create a VPC Service Controls perimeter around BigQuery and Cloud Storage, configure an organization policy blocking public buckets, and route Security Command Center findings to a Pub/Sub topic.

gcp-architect.title: Infrastructure as Code with Terraform on GCP
gcp-architect.description: Manage GCP infrastructure as code using Terraform with the Google provider. Use Cloud Build for automated apply pipelines and Config Validator for policy enforcement.
topics: Cloud Build triggers for Terraform CI/CD

resources.label: Automating Infrastructure on GCP with Terraform

gcp-architect.challenge: Write Terraform modules to deploy a GKE cluster, Cloud SQL instance, and VPC, store state in Cloud Storage, and trigger automated plans and applies from a Cloud Build pipeline on pull request.

gcp-architect.title: Serverless and App Modernization
gcp-architect.description: Build event-driven and HTTP-triggered serverless workloads on GCP using Cloud Run and Cloud Functions. Connect services with Eventarc and integrate with Firebase for mobile backends.
topics: Cloud Run containers and scaling to zero
gcp-architect.challenge: Deploy a stateless API on Cloud Run with minimum instances set to 1, trigger a Cloud Function via Eventarc on Cloud Storage upload events, and wire them together in a processing workflow.

gcp-architect.title: Reliability and Observability
gcp-architect.description: Define and track SLOs for GCP workloads using Cloud Monitoring and create error budgets for reliability targets. Implement distributed tracing and structured logging at scale.
topics: Cloud Logging log sinks and exclusions
gcp-architect.challenge: Define SLOs for a Cloud Run service, create an error budget burn rate alert, export logs to BigQuery for long-term analysis, and build a Cloud Monitoring dashboard with latency percentiles.

gcp-architect.title: Cost Optimization and Resource Management
gcp-architect.description: Analyze and optimize GCP spending using billing exports, recommender insights, and committed use discounts. Implement budget alerts and spend controls at the project and folder level.
topics: Cost breakdown by project service label
Budget alerts and programmatic spend controls
gcp-architect.challenge: Export billing data to BigQuery, write SQL queries to find the top five cost drivers, apply committed use discounts to a compute fleet, and set up hierarchical budget alerts at the folder level.

gcp-architect.title: Professional Cloud Architect and Enterprise Patterns
gcp-architect.description: Design enterprise GCP environments using the Cloud Foundation Toolkit and Anthos for multi-cluster and hybrid workloads. Apply the Google Cloud Architecture Framework to all five pillars.
topics: GCP landing zone with Cloud Foundation Toolkit
Anthos Config Management and Policy Controller
Anthos Service Mesh for multi-cluster traffic
gcp-architect.challenge: Deploy a GCP landing zone with the Cloud Foundation Toolkit covering three environments, attach Anthos Config Management to enforce policies across all clusters, and conduct a Well-Architected review.

serverless-dev.title: Serverless Fundamentals
serverless-dev.description: Understand the serverless computing model, its trade-offs, and how AWS Lambda executes functions. Create and test Lambda functions with different triggers and execution roles.
topics: IAM execution roles and least privilege
serverless-dev.challenge: Write a Lambda function in Python that responds to an S3 upload event, extracts metadata from the file, and writes it to CloudWatch Logs with structured JSON formatting.

serverless-dev.title: API Gateway and HTTP APIs
serverless-dev.description: Build RESTful and HTTP APIs using API Gateway integrated with Lambda. Configure authentication, CORS, request validation, and usage plans.
topics: REST vs HTTP API in API Gateway
serverless-dev.challenge: Build an HTTP API with three routes backed by separate Lambda functions, add a Lambda authorizer for JWT validation, enable access logging to CloudWatch, and test the API from Postman.

serverless-dev.title: Data Storage for Serverless
serverless-dev.description: Design serverless-compatible data storage patterns using DynamoDB single-table design and S3. Understand connection reuse patterns and avoid cold-start penalties from connection pools.
topics: DynamoDB Streams for change data capture
S3 event notifications and presigned URLs
Connection reuse in Lambda for external services
SSM Parameter Store and Secrets Manager
serverless-dev.challenge: Design a DynamoDB single-table schema for a multi-tenant app, implement a Lambda that writes and reads using composite keys, and generate presigned S3 URLs from a Lambda for file uploads.

serverless-dev.title: AWS SAM and Local Development
serverless-dev.description: Use the AWS Serverless Application Model framework to define, test, and deploy serverless applications. Run Lambda and API Gateway locally using SAM CLI and LocalStack.
topics: sam build sam local invoke and start-api

resources.label: Serverless Applications with AWS SAM Course

serverless-dev.challenge: Convert an existing Lambda and API Gateway setup into a full SAM application, add sam local invoke tests with event payloads, and set up a SAM pipeline that deploys to staging and prod.

serverless-dev.title: Event-Driven Patterns with SQS and SNS
serverless-dev.description: Design event-driven serverless architectures using SQS for reliable queuing, SNS for fan-out, and EventBridge for event routing. Handle failures with dead-letter queues and retry policies.
topics: Lambda event source mapping for SQS
SNS fan-out to multiple SQS queues
serverless-dev.challenge: Build an order processing flow with an SNS topic that fans out to an SQS order queue and SQS notification queue, each processed by a separate Lambda, with DLQs for failed messages.

serverless-dev.title: Orchestration with Step Functions
serverless-dev.description: Orchestrate multi-step workflows and handle complex business logic using AWS Step Functions state machines. Implement error handling, retries, and parallel branches.
topics: Step Functions Standard vs Express workflows
Task catch retry and error handling
serverless-dev.challenge: Create a Step Functions state machine for a document approval workflow with parallel reviewer tasks, a timeout wait state, and error handling that notifies via SNS when the workflow fails.

serverless-dev.title: Performance and Cold Starts
serverless-dev.description: Diagnose and mitigate Lambda cold start latency using provisioned concurrency, SnapStart for Java, and Lambda Layers. Profile function memory and CPU allocation for cost-performance balance.
topics: Lambda cold start causes and measurement
serverless-dev.challenge: Profile a Lambda function using Lambda Power Tuning, identify the optimal memory setting, add provisioned concurrency for peak traffic windows, and measure cold start vs warm start latency.

serverless-dev.title: Security for Serverless Applications
serverless-dev.description: Secure serverless applications with fine-grained IAM permissions, Cognito user pools for authentication, and WAF for API protection. Manage secrets without hardcoding credentials.
topics: Cognito user pools and identity pools
WAF rules and managed rule groups on API Gateway
serverless-dev.challenge: Add Cognito user pool authentication to an API Gateway, configure a Lambda authorizer to validate tokens, attach a WAF with rate limiting to the API, and rotate a database secret with a Lambda rotation function.

serverless-dev.title: Observability and Debugging
serverless-dev.description: Implement end-to-end distributed tracing and structured observability using AWS X-Ray and Lambda Powertools. Create dashboards and alarms for serverless operational visibility.
topics: AWS X-Ray tracing and service maps
AWS Lambda Powertools for Python and TypeScript
Canary deployments and rollback for Lambda
serverless-dev.challenge: Instrument a multi-Lambda application with X-Ray active tracing and Lambda Powertools, create a CloudWatch dashboard with p99 latency and error rate, and set up a canary deployment alias.

serverless-dev.title: Production Serverless Architecture
serverless-dev.description: Architect production-ready serverless platforms with multi-stage deployments, traffic shifting, and event-driven integration patterns. Apply the AWS Serverless Application Lens of the Well-Architected Framework.
serverless-dev.challenge: Conduct a Well-Architected serverless review on an existing application, remediate all high-risk findings, implement traffic shifting on a critical Lambda alias, and document the architecture decision records.

cloud-native.title: Containers and Docker Fundamentals
cloud-native.description: Build, run, and manage containerized applications using Docker. Understand image layers, multi-stage builds, and container networking basics.
topics: Docker Compose for local multi-service apps
cloud-native.challenge: Write a multi-stage Dockerfile for a Node.js app that uses a build stage and a minimal production image, run it with Docker Compose alongside a PostgreSQL container, and push the image to Docker Hub.

cloud-native.title: Kubernetes Fundamentals
cloud-native.description: Deploy and manage containerized workloads on Kubernetes using core objects like Pods, Deployments, Services, and ConfigMaps. Understand the control plane and worker node architecture.
topics: Kubernetes architecture control plane and nodes
cloud-native.challenge: Deploy a three-tier application (frontend, API, database) on a local Kubernetes cluster using minikube, expose each tier with appropriate Service types, and use Secrets for database credentials.

cloud-native.title: Helm and Package Management
cloud-native.description: Package Kubernetes applications as Helm charts with parameterized values for different environments. Manage release lifecycle with upgrades, rollbacks, and hooks.
topics: Helm chart structure templates and values
cloud-native.challenge: Package an existing multi-service Kubernetes application as a Helm chart with separate values files for dev and prod, add a pre-upgrade hook that runs database migrations, and publish the chart to an OCI registry.

cloud-native.title: Kubernetes Networking and Ingress
cloud-native.description: Configure external access to Kubernetes services using Ingress controllers and implement network policies for microsegmentation. Understand CNI plugins and DNS resolution.
topics: Ingress rules TLS and path routing
cloud-native.challenge: Deploy nginx-ingress with TLS termination, create NetworkPolicies that allow frontend to talk to API but deny direct frontend to database traffic, and verify with kubectl exec connectivity tests.

cloud-native.title: Kubernetes Storage and Stateful Workloads
cloud-native.description: Deploy stateful applications on Kubernetes using StatefulSets, PersistentVolumeClaims, and CSI storage drivers. Understand storage class provisioners and volume expansion.
cloud-native.challenge: Deploy a PostgreSQL StatefulSet with a persistent volume claim, configure a CSI driver for dynamic provisioning, take a volume snapshot, and restore from it to validate the backup process.

cloud-native.title: Service Mesh with Istio
cloud-native.description: Implement a service mesh using Istio to provide traffic management, mutual TLS, and observability without changing application code. Configure traffic shifting and circuit breaking.
topics: Istio architecture control plane and data plane
cloud-native.challenge: Install Istio on a cluster, enable sidecar injection, configure a canary release shifting 10 percent traffic to a new version, enable mutual TLS cluster-wide, and verify mTLS with istioctl.

cloud-native.title: GitOps and Continuous Delivery
cloud-native.description: Implement GitOps workflows using ArgoCD or Flux to declaratively manage Kubernetes deployments from Git repositories. Use Kustomize overlays for environment-specific configurations.
topics: Secrets management with Sealed Secrets or ESO
cloud-native.challenge: Set up ArgoCD with the app-of-apps pattern, manage three microservices each with dev and prod overlays using Kustomize, and configure Argo Rollouts for a blue-green deployment strategy.

cloud-native.title: Security and Policy Enforcement
cloud-native.description: Enforce security policies on Kubernetes workloads using OPA Gatekeeper admission webhooks and runtime threat detection with Falco. Implement RBAC and pod security standards.
topics: Kubernetes RBAC roles and role bindings
Image scanning in CI with Trivy
Supply chain security with Sigstore Cosign
cloud-native.challenge: Deploy OPA Gatekeeper with constraints that require resource limits on all pods and block privileged containers, install Falco with a custom rule that alerts on exec into a production pod.

cloud-native.title: Observability for Kubernetes
cloud-native.description: Build full-stack observability for cloud native applications using the Prometheus and Grafana ecosystem, OpenTelemetry for instrumentation, and Loki for log aggregation.
topics: ServiceMonitor and PodMonitor with Prometheus Operator
Distributed tracing with Tempo or Jaeger
cloud-native.challenge: Deploy the kube-prometheus-stack, instrument a Go service with OpenTelemetry to emit traces to Tempo, forward logs to Loki, and create a Grafana dashboard showing the RED method metrics.

cloud-native.title: Production Cloud Native Platform Engineering
cloud-native.description: Build and operate a production-grade internal developer platform on Kubernetes with self-service capabilities, golden paths, and CNCF project integrations. Apply cloud native maturity model principles.
cloud-native.challenge: Deploy Backstage with a software catalog populated from your git repositories, create a golden path template that provisions a new microservice with CI/CD, monitoring, and GitOps configuration in one click.

finops-engineer.title: FinOps Fundamentals
finops-engineer.description: Understand the FinOps framework, personas, and the crawl-walk-run maturity model. Learn how cloud billing works across AWS, Azure, and GCP at a conceptual level.
topics: Cloud billing models on-demand reserved spot
Variable cost model vs fixed cost
Unit economics and cost per transaction
finops-engineer.challenge: Map your organization cloud spend to the FinOps capability model, identify which phase you are in for each domain, and write a 1-page plan to reach the next maturity level.

finops-engineer.title: Cloud Billing and Cost Visibility
finops-engineer.description: Set up comprehensive cost visibility across cloud accounts using native billing tools and BigQuery exports. Identify the major cost drivers and understand how services are priced.
topics: AWS Cost Explorer filters and grouping
finops-engineer.challenge: Enable billing export for all three major clouds, write a BigQuery query to compare month-over-month cost changes by service, and build a unified cost dashboard in Looker Studio.

finops-engineer.title: Tagging and Cost Allocation
finops-engineer.description: Design and enforce a consistent tagging taxonomy across cloud resources for accurate cost attribution. Use policy enforcement to ensure tag compliance at resource creation.
topics: Tagging taxonomy design team env cost-center
GCP label requirements via Organization Policy
finops-engineer.challenge: Define a 5-tag taxonomy, enforce it with AWS Tag Policies and Azure Policy, write a script that reports tag coverage percentage per account, and remediate untagged resources automatically.

finops-engineer.title: Commitments and Rate Optimization
finops-engineer.description: Analyze usage patterns and purchase appropriate savings commitments to reduce on-demand rates by 30 to 70 percent. Model the trade-off between flexibility and savings.
topics: AWS savings plans compute vs instance
AWS reserved instances types and terms
Azure reserved VM instances and AHB
GCP committed use discounts 1 and 3 year
finops-engineer.challenge: Analyze 6 months of EC2 usage, model coverage scenarios at 60, 70, and 80 percent, purchase the optimal savings plan mix, and set up alerts when utilization drops below the target threshold.

finops-engineer.title: Right-Sizing and Waste Elimination
finops-engineer.description: Identify and eliminate cloud waste through right-sizing under-utilized instances, deleting idle resources, and eliminating zombie workloads. Use cloud native recommenders and custom scripts.

resources.label: FinOps Open Cost and Usage Spec

finops-engineer.challenge: Run Compute Optimizer across an AWS environment, categorize findings by effort and impact, right-size the top 10 instances, and write a Lambda that automatically deletes snapshots older than 90 days.

finops-engineer.title: Budget Management and Governance
finops-engineer.description: Implement budget guardrails with automated alerting and cost anomaly detection to prevent unexpected spend. Design a governance framework for cloud cost approvals.
topics: Azure budget alerts and action groups
GCP budget alerts and programmatic response
finops-engineer.challenge: Set up AWS Budgets for each team with 80 and 100 percent threshold alerts, enable cost anomaly detection with a 10 percent threshold, and configure a budget action that stops an EC2 instance on breach.

finops-engineer.title: Unit Economics and Metrics
finops-engineer.description: Build unit economic metrics that connect cloud costs to business value such as cost per customer, per API call, or per transaction. Automate metric collection and reporting.
topics: FOCUS FinOps Open Cost and Usage Specification
Connecting cost data to business metrics
Cost per environment and per feature
finops-engineer.challenge: Define three unit cost metrics for a SaaS product, build a pipeline that joins billing data with application metrics in BigQuery, and publish weekly unit cost trends to a shared Grafana dashboard.

finops-engineer.title: Kubernetes and Container Cost Allocation
finops-engineer.description: Allocate Kubernetes cluster costs to teams and namespaces using OpenCost or Kubecost. Understand the challenge of shared resources and bin-packing efficiency.
finops-engineer.challenge: Deploy OpenCost on a Kubernetes cluster, configure namespace cost allocation by team label, identify the top three inefficient workloads, and implement resource requests and limits to improve bin-packing.

finops-engineer.title: FinOps Tooling and Automation
finops-engineer.description: Integrate FinOps tooling into engineering workflows by shifting cost left with Infracost in pull requests and automating optimization actions via Lambda or Cloud Functions.
topics: Infracost in CI/CD pull request comments
Custom cost optimization rules and automation
Reporting pipelines with dbt and BigQuery
finops-engineer.challenge: Integrate Infracost into a GitHub Actions pipeline that comments estimated monthly cost on every Terraform pull request, and build a Lambda that emails teams weekly with their top cost reduction opportunities.

finops-engineer.title: FinOps at Scale and Organizational Maturity
finops-engineer.description: Lead an organization to FinOps run-phase maturity with executive reporting, cross-functional collaboration, and continuous optimization culture. Align cloud spend with business outcomes.
topics: FinOps run phase capabilities and KPIs
Cloud economics and total cost of ownership
Establishing a cloud center of excellence
Forecasting and financial planning for cloud
finops-engineer.challenge: Build a quarterly business review deck with cloud unit economics, forecast next quarter spend within 10 percent accuracy, propose a commitment purchase plan, and present it to a simulated executive audience.

multi-cloud-architect.title: Multi-Cloud Strategy Fundamentals
multi-cloud-architect.description: Understand why organizations adopt multi-cloud strategies and the associated trade-offs around complexity, vendor lock-in, and operational overhead. Compare core service equivalents across providers.
topics: Multi-cloud vs poly-cloud vs hybrid cloud
multi-cloud-architect.challenge: Map the equivalent services for compute, storage, database, and messaging across AWS, Azure, and GCP, and write a decision matrix with criteria for when to prefer each provider.

multi-cloud-architect.title: Terraform for Multi-Cloud IaC
multi-cloud-architect.description: Manage infrastructure across AWS, Azure, and GCP from a single Terraform codebase using multiple provider configurations. Structure workspaces and modules for multi-cloud reusability.
topics: Remote state with locking across clouds
Terraform Cloud or Atlantis for team workflows
multi-cloud-architect.challenge: Write a Terraform root module that deploys an identical VPC and compute instance on AWS and Azure simultaneously, using provider aliases, and stores state in separate backends.

multi-cloud-architect.title: Multi-Cloud Networking
multi-cloud-architect.description: Design network connectivity between AWS, Azure, and GCP environments using VPN tunnels, private interconnects, and SD-WAN solutions. Implement consistent security policies across cloud perimeters.
topics: VPN between AWS and Azure and GCP
multi-cloud-architect.challenge: Establish VPN connectivity between an AWS VPC and an Azure VNet, configure BGP route exchange, and validate that a VM in each cloud can ping the other using private IP addresses.

multi-cloud-architect.title: Multi-Cloud Identity and Security
multi-cloud-architect.description: Federate identities across cloud providers using a central IdP such as Okta or Entra ID. Implement consistent RBAC patterns and secret management across AWS, Azure, and GCP.
topics: Identity federation with SAML and OIDC
Okta or Entra ID as central IdP
multi-cloud-architect.challenge: Configure Okta as the IdP for AWS IAM Identity Center, Azure Entra ID, and GCP Workforce Identity Federation, then test single sign-on to all three consoles with the same user credentials.

multi-cloud-architect.title: Multi-Cloud Kubernetes
multi-cloud-architect.description: Operate Kubernetes clusters across AWS EKS, Azure AKS, and GCP GKE with consistent tooling. Use Cluster API or a management plane to provision and lifecycle clusters uniformly.
topics: EKS vs AKS vs GKE feature comparison
Cluster API provider for each cloud
multi-cloud-architect.challenge: Provision one cluster each on EKS, AKS, and GKE using Cluster API, deploy the same application to all three via ArgoCD ApplicationSets, and verify cross-cluster health from a single Grafana dashboard.

multi-cloud-architect.title: Multi-Cloud Data and Storage Patterns
multi-cloud-architect.description: Design portable data architectures that span multiple cloud object stores using abstraction layers and open formats. Implement cross-cloud replication for disaster recovery.
topics: Object storage comparison S3 Blob GCS
Open table formats Delta Lake Iceberg
multi-cloud-architect.challenge: Set up bi-directional replication between S3 and Azure Blob Storage using Rclone, write data in Delta Lake format that can be queried from both AWS Athena and Azure Synapse.

multi-cloud-architect.title: Multi-Cloud Observability
multi-cloud-architect.description: Centralize observability across multi-cloud environments using OpenTelemetry for vendor-neutral instrumentation and a unified monitoring backend. Correlate metrics logs and traces across providers.
topics: Centralized Prometheus with Thanos or Cortex
Grafana unified dashboards for all clouds
multi-cloud-architect.challenge: Deploy OpenTelemetry collectors in AWS and Azure, forward all telemetry to a central Grafana stack, and create a single dashboard showing request rates error rates and latency for services running in both clouds.

multi-cloud-architect.title: Multi-Cloud Cost Management
multi-cloud-architect.description: Build a unified view of cloud spend across AWS, Azure, and GCP using the FOCUS specification and multi-cloud cost management platforms. Implement consistent tagging and allocation.
topics: FOCUS specification for normalized billing data
Cost anomaly detection across all clouds
multi-cloud-architect.challenge: Normalize billing exports from AWS, Azure, and GCP into the FOCUS schema, load them into BigQuery, and build a dashboard showing total multi-cloud spend by team with month-over-month variance.

multi-cloud-architect.title: Disaster Recovery and Business Continuity
multi-cloud-architect.description: Design multi-cloud disaster recovery architectures that span providers to achieve cloud-provider-level resilience. Test failover between AWS and Azure with automated runbooks.
topics: Multi-cloud DR patterns active-active vs active-passive
multi-cloud-architect.challenge: Set up a Velero backup pipeline for a Kubernetes workload replicating to both an S3 bucket and an Azure Blob container, implement DNS failover between AWS and Azure, and run a full DR drill.

multi-cloud-architect.title: Enterprise Multi-Cloud Governance
multi-cloud-architect.description: Govern a large-scale multi-cloud environment with policy-as-code, cloud security posture management, and automated compliance reporting across all cloud providers.
topics: Policy as code with OPA across clouds
CSPM tools for unified security posture
Terraform Enterprise or Spacelift for governance
multi-cloud-architect.challenge: Deploy Cloud Custodian policies that run across AWS and Azure to enforce encryption at rest, remediate public storage buckets, and generate a unified compliance report mapped to CIS benchmark controls.

cloud-migration.title: Migration Fundamentals and Strategy
cloud-migration.description: Understand the cloud migration journey using the 7Rs strategy framework and learn how to build a migration business case. Identify migration drivers and stakeholder concerns.
cloud-migration.challenge: Analyze a fictional on-premises application portfolio of 20 apps, classify each using the 7Rs framework, and build a migration business case with a 3-year TCO comparison.

cloud-migration.title: Discovery and Portfolio Assessment
cloud-migration.description: Conduct application portfolio discovery using automated tools to collect inventory, performance data, and dependency maps. Produce a rationalized portfolio view for migration planning.
topics: AWS Application Discovery Service agent vs agentless
cloud-migration.challenge: Deploy AWS Application Discovery Service agents on a lab environment, collect 2 weeks of performance and dependency data, and produce a migration assessment report with recommended wave groupings.

cloud-migration.title: Landing Zone and Foundation Setup
cloud-migration.description: Build the cloud landing zone foundation that target workloads will migrate into. Establish networking, identity, logging, and security baselines before the first migration wave.
cloud-migration.challenge: Deploy an AWS landing zone with Control Tower including three organizational units, a shared services VPC, Direct Connect simulation with VPN, and Active Directory integration.

cloud-migration.title: Lift and Shift Migration
cloud-migration.description: Execute rehost migrations moving virtual machines to the cloud with minimal changes using AWS Application Migration Service and Azure Migrate. Perform cutover with minimal downtime.
topics: AWS Application Migration Service MGN setup
Azure Migrate VM assessment and migration
cloud-migration.challenge: Migrate three VMs from a VMware environment to AWS using AWS MGN, perform test cutovers to validate functionality, execute a production cutover during a maintenance window, and decommission the source.

cloud-migration.title: Database Migration
cloud-migration.description: Migrate on-premises databases to cloud managed database services using AWS DMS and the Schema Conversion Tool. Handle heterogeneous migrations from Oracle and SQL Server to open source engines.
topics: Schema Conversion Tool for heterogeneous migrations
Database cutover and minimal downtime strategies
cloud-migration.challenge: Migrate an Oracle database to Amazon Aurora PostgreSQL using the Schema Conversion Tool for schema translation and DMS for full load and CDC replication, validate row counts, and cutover.

cloud-migration.title: Replatforming and Modernization
cloud-migration.description: Replatform applications to take advantage of managed cloud services without full refactoring. Move from self-managed databases and servers to PaaS offerings for reduced operational burden.
topics: Migrating to managed databases RDS Aurora
cloud-migration.challenge: Take a LAMP stack application, containerize it with Docker, deploy it to AWS ECS Fargate, migrate the MySQL database to RDS, and verify all functionality with automated integration tests.

cloud-migration.title: Refactoring and Application Modernization
cloud-migration.description: Apply the strangler fig pattern to incrementally decompose monolithic applications into cloud-native microservices and serverless functions. Prioritize decomposition by business capability.
topics: Strangler fig pattern for monolith decomposition
API gateway as strangler entry point
cloud-migration.challenge: Apply the strangler fig pattern to extract one domain from a monolith, implement it as a serverless microservice, route traffic to it via API Gateway, and keep the legacy path as fallback.

cloud-migration.title: Data Center Exit and Decommissioning
cloud-migration.description: Plan and execute the final data center exit including decommissioning physical assets, terminating colocation contracts, and validating all workloads are stable in the cloud.
topics: Data center exit planning and timeline
cloud-migration.challenge: Build a data center exit tracker spreadsheet with status for all 20 migrated apps, define go/no-go criteria for decommissioning, and write a runbook for the final shutdown weekend.

cloud-migration.title: Post-Migration Optimization
cloud-migration.description: Optimize migrated workloads for cloud-native performance and cost efficiency after migration is complete. Conduct Well-Architected reviews and right-size resources based on actual usage data.
topics: Reserved instance and savings plan purchases
Implementing cloud-native patterns in migrated apps
cloud-migration.challenge: Run a Well-Architected review on three migrated workloads, address all high-risk items within 30 days, right-size 80 percent of EC2 instances based on 2 weeks of CloudWatch data.

cloud-migration.title: Migration Program Management at Scale
cloud-migration.description: Lead a large-scale cloud migration program across hundreds of applications with wave planning, governance, and a cloud center of excellence. Measure and report migration velocity and outcomes.
cloud-migration.challenge: Design a 12-month migration program plan for a 200-application portfolio, define wave groupings by dependency and risk, create a RACI for the cloud COE, and build a migration velocity dashboard.

edge-engineer.title: Edge Computing Fundamentals
edge-engineer.description: Understand the edge computing model, how CDNs work, and why moving computation closer to users reduces latency. Compare centralized cloud with edge and CDN delivery.
topics: CDN points of presence and caching
edge-engineer.challenge: Set up a Cloudflare free account, proxy a domain through it, configure caching rules for static assets with long TTLs, and measure TTFB improvement with and without Cloudflare using WebPageTest.

edge-engineer.title: Cloudflare Workers Basics
edge-engineer.description: Write and deploy serverless JavaScript functions at the Cloudflare edge using Workers. Understand the Workers runtime, request lifecycle, and how it differs from Node.js.
topics: Fetch event handler and request response
Wrangler CLI for local dev and deployment
edge-engineer.challenge: Write a Cloudflare Worker that intercepts requests, adds custom headers, rewrites URLs based on geolocation, and A/B tests two origin URLs by splitting traffic 50/50 based on a cookie.

edge-engineer.title: Edge Storage and State
edge-engineer.description: Persist data at the edge using Cloudflare KV, Durable Objects, R2 object storage, and D1 SQLite databases. Understand consistency trade-offs of eventually consistent KV vs strongly consistent Durable Objects.
topics: R2 storage vs S3 cost comparison
D1 SQLite at the edge queries and migrations
edge-engineer.challenge: Build a real-time collaborative counter using Durable Objects that handles concurrent increments without conflicts, stores results in KV for fast reads, and persists history in D1.

edge-engineer.title: WebAssembly at the Edge
edge-engineer.description: Compile Rust or AssemblyScript to WebAssembly and run it inside Cloudflare Workers for near-native performance at the edge. Understand WASM module instantiation and memory management.
topics: Compiling Rust to WASM with wasm-pack
WASM in Cloudflare Workers and Workers WASM bindings
edge-engineer.challenge: Write a Rust function that performs image resizing, compile it to WASM, load it inside a Cloudflare Worker, and benchmark it against a pure JavaScript implementation for a 1MB image.

edge-engineer.title: Edge Functions on Vercel and Netlify
edge-engineer.description: Deploy edge middleware and functions on Vercel and Netlify platforms to personalize content and handle authentication at the CDN layer. Integrate with popular front-end frameworks.
topics: Vercel Edge Functions vs serverless functions
Netlify Edge Functions with Deno runtime
A/B testing at the edge with feature flags
edge-engineer.challenge: Build a Next.js app with edge middleware that redirects users to locale-specific pages based on Accept-Language header, validates JWT tokens at the edge, and blocks specified countries via geolocation.

edge-engineer.title: Edge Security and WAF
edge-engineer.description: Protect applications at the edge using Web Application Firewall rules, DDoS mitigation, and bot management. Implement Zero Trust access policies with Cloudflare Access.
topics: WAF rule sets and custom rules
Bot score and bot fight mode
mTLS at the edge for API security
edge-engineer.challenge: Configure a WAF ruleset blocking OWASP top 10 categories, implement rate limiting at 100 requests per minute per IP, set up a Cloudflare Access policy requiring SSO for an internal dashboard.

edge-engineer.title: CDN Performance Optimization
edge-engineer.description: Tune CDN cache rules for maximum offload and implement edge-side image optimization and format conversion. Measure and improve Core Web Vitals scores using edge capabilities.
topics: Cache key design and variation rules
Edge-side image transformation and WebP conversion
Cache purging strategies and cache tags
Core Web Vitals and edge optimization
edge-engineer.challenge: Audit a website with Google PageSpeed Insights, implement edge image optimization with Cloudflare Polish, configure granular cache rules by content type, and improve LCP score by at least 30 percent.

edge-engineer.title: Edge AI and Inference
edge-engineer.description: Run machine learning inference at the edge using Cloudflare Workers AI and ONNX runtime to reduce latency and avoid sending data to centralized inference servers.
topics: Edge AI inference trade-offs vs cloud
Text classification and embedding at edge
edge-engineer.challenge: Build a Cloudflare Worker that classifies incoming request text as spam or not-spam using Workers AI, caches results in KV for repeated queries, and blocks requests classified as spam.

edge-engineer.title: IoT and Edge Protocols
edge-engineer.description: Extend edge computing to IoT devices using MQTT, WebSockets, and cloud-managed edge runtimes. Design architectures that process sensor data locally before syncing to the cloud.
topics: MQTT protocol pub/sub and QoS levels
AWS Greengrass local compute and ML
Azure IoT Edge modules and routes
edge-engineer.challenge: Deploy an AWS Greengrass core device that subscribes to MQTT sensor messages, processes anomalies locally using a Lambda function, and syncs only flagged events to AWS IoT Core in the cloud.

edge-engineer.title: Production Edge Architecture
edge-engineer.description: Design and operate production edge platforms with multi-CDN failover, edge observability, and sophisticated request orchestration logic. Apply edge-first architecture patterns at scale.
topics: Edge observability with Workers Analytics Engine
Request coalescing and cache stampede prevention
Cloudflare Pages and Workers combined deployments
Edge configuration as code with Wrangler and Terraform
SLAs and incident response for edge platforms

resources.label: Building for the Edge Architecture Guide

edge-engineer.challenge: Build a production edge platform with Terraform-managed Cloudflare configuration, custom analytics events to Workers Analytics Engine, multi-origin failover, and a runbook for edge incident response.

observability-engineer.title: Observability Fundamentals
observability-engineer.description: Understand the three pillars of observability and how they differ from traditional monitoring. Learn the difference between black-box and white-box monitoring and when to apply each.
topics: MTTD and MTTR improvement with observability
observability-engineer.challenge: Instrument a simple web application to emit request rate, error rate, and latency as Prometheus metrics, structured JSON logs, and traces, and visualize all three in a single Grafana dashboard.

observability-engineer.title: Prometheus Metrics
observability-engineer.description: Set up Prometheus to scrape metrics from applications and infrastructure exporters. Write PromQL queries for aggregation and alerting, and configure Alertmanager notification routing.
topics: Four metric types counter gauge histogram summary
observability-engineer.challenge: Deploy Prometheus with node-exporter and a custom application exporter, write PromQL queries for p50 p95 p99 latency, create an alert rule for high error rate, and route alerts to a Slack channel.

observability-engineer.title: Grafana Dashboards and Alerting
observability-engineer.description: Build operational dashboards in Grafana with best-practice panel choices, variables, and drill-down links. Configure Grafana-native alerting with multi-datasource support.
topics: Grafana panel types and when to use each
Alert contact points and notification policies
Grafana dashboard best practices and anti-patterns
observability-engineer.challenge: Build a service dashboard with RED method panels using template variables for service and environment, add a drill-down link to traces, define two alert rules with PagerDuty routing.

observability-engineer.title: Distributed Tracing
observability-engineer.description: Implement distributed tracing to follow requests across microservices. Use OpenTelemetry SDK to instrument applications and store traces in Jaeger or Grafana Tempo.
topics: Distributed tracing concepts spans and traces
Grafana Tempo for cost-effective trace storage
observability-engineer.challenge: Instrument a 3-service application with OpenTelemetry, propagate trace context through HTTP headers and message queues, send traces to Grafana Tempo, and correlate a 500ms latency spike to a specific database query.

observability-engineer.title: Log Management with Loki
observability-engineer.description: Aggregate and query application and infrastructure logs using Grafana Loki with Promtail agents. Write LogQL queries for filtering, parsing, and creating metrics from log data.
topics: Promtail scrape configs and pipeline stages
Log parsing with regex and JSON stage
Correlating logs with metrics and traces in Grafana
observability-engineer.challenge: Deploy Loki with Promtail to collect logs from a Kubernetes cluster, write a LogQL query that extracts HTTP status codes from nginx logs, and create a Grafana panel showing error rate derived from log data.

observability-engineer.title: OpenTelemetry Collector and Pipelines
observability-engineer.description: Deploy and configure the OpenTelemetry Collector as a central telemetry pipeline for receiving, processing, and exporting metrics, logs, and traces to multiple backends.
topics: OTel Collector architecture receivers processors exporters
Collector deployment patterns agent vs gateway
observability-engineer.challenge: Deploy an OTel Collector that receives traces from applications via OTLP, enriches them with Kubernetes pod metadata using the k8sattributes processor, and fans out to both Tempo and Datadog simultaneously.

observability-engineer.title: SLOs and Error Budgets
observability-engineer.description: Define service level objectives and automate error budget burn rate alerts using Prometheus recording rules and tools like Sloth. Align on-call practices with error budget consumption.
topics: SLI SLO SLA definitions and hierarchy
Choosing meaningful SLIs for user journeys
observability-engineer.challenge: Define SLOs for availability and latency for three services, implement them with Sloth generating Prometheus recording rules, set up 1-hour and 6-hour burn rate alerts, and create an error budget dashboard.

observability-engineer.title: Datadog and Commercial Observability
observability-engineer.description: Implement end-to-end observability using Datadog APM, infrastructure monitoring, and log management. Configure Datadog monitors, dashboards, and synthetic tests.
observability-engineer.challenge: Instrument a containerized application with Datadog APM using auto-instrumentation, create a service map, set up a monitor for p99 latency exceeding 500ms, and write a synthetic test simulating the checkout flow.

observability-engineer.title: Observability for Kubernetes
observability-engineer.description: Build comprehensive Kubernetes observability covering node, pod, and control plane metrics with the kube-prometheus-stack. Create cluster health dashboards and capacity planning views.
observability-engineer.challenge: Deploy kube-prometheus-stack, create a capacity planning dashboard showing node CPU and memory headroom, set up alerts for nodes above 80 percent usage, and configure Kubernetes event forwarding to Loki.

observability-engineer.title: Production Observability Platform Engineering
observability-engineer.description: Design and operate a production-grade multi-tenant observability platform with long-term metric storage using Thanos or Grafana Mimir, and manage the full stack as code.
topics: Observability as code with Terraform and Jsonnet

resources.label: Grafana Loki Tempo Mimir Stack Course

observability-engineer.challenge: Deploy a production Grafana Mimir cluster with S3-backed long-term storage, onboard three teams with tenant isolation, manage all dashboards and alerts as code in a Git repository with CI/CD deployment.
